Sect1.Title: Operator Manual
Sect1.Model: Pro 6019 H Cab & Cowl Pro 6028 H Cab & Cowl Pro 6035 H Cab & Cowl Pro 6042 H Cab & Cowl Pro 6048 H Cab & Cowl Pro 6048XP H Cab & Cowl Pro 6041 H CabPro 6041 H Cab Pro 6041 Cab - POL

ListItem.Para: Welcome to the family of VE Commercial Vehicles Ltd. We take this opportunity to congratulate and thank you for purchasing EICHER vehicles.

It is our constant endeavor to provide you with products that offer excellent performance. We have also setup an extensive, very well equipped network of dealers & authorized service centers across the country.
EICHER Vehicles which you have purchased is of high quality & result of continuous innovation and research. We are privileged to have you as our customer.
EICHER Vehicles are manufactured based on the market requirement & customer satisfaction. We wish you much pleasure with your EICHER vehicles and pleasant motoring at all times.

Sect2.Title: Foreword
Sect2.Para: Thank you for purchasing an EICHER vehicle.
This Operator’s manual comprises of many important and useful sections, the sections are.

ListItem.Para: The first section contains Introduction of EICHER Vehicle.
The second section contains Know your vehicle.
The third section contains Warranty offered by VE Commercial Vehicles Ltd.
The fourth section contains the Maintenance.
The fifth section contains Handling emergencies.
The sixth section contains Body building guidelines.
The seventh section contains EICHER Driver training school
The eighth section contains List of EICHER Authorised Networks.

Sect2.Para: To keep the vehicle in top operating condition at all times and use it for an extended period of time, make sure that this handbook is read thorough, carefully.

ParaHead.Title: Keep this Operator’s manual in your vehicle for ready reference.

Sect2.Para: If any questions arise concerning the contents of the Operator’s manual, please contact our Authorised Dealer for clarifications.

ListItem.Para: The explanations and photographs may contain some differences from your vehicle because of change in specifications incorporated from time to time as a result of continuous innovation and research.
All illustrations shown in this manual are indicative only.
Heavy Motor Vehicle (HMV) driving license with appropriate validity is required for driving EICHER vehicles.

Most accidents that involve product operation, maintenance and repair are caused by failure to observe basic safety rules or precautions. An accident can often be avoided by recognizing potentially hazardous situations before an accident occurs. A person must be alert to potential hazards. This person should also have the necessary training, Skills & tools to perform these functions properly.
Your safety and the safety of others is very important. So, operating the vehicle safely is an important responsibility.
Obey traffic rules & ensure that before operating the vehicle all must know the traffic signs.
Do not closely follow a large vehicle through an intersection with stop lights. You can’t see if the light has changed.
Improper operation, Lubrication, maintenance or repair of this product can be dangerous and could results in injury or fatality.
Do not operate or perform any lubrication, maintenance or repair on this product, until you have read and understood the operation, lubrication, maintenance & repair information.

To help you make informed decisions about safety, we have provided operating procedures and other information's in this manual. This information alerts you to potential hazards that could hurt you or others.
These signal words mean Of course, it is not practical or possible to warn you about all the hazards associated with operating or maintaining your vehicle. You must use your own good judgment.
The hazards are identified by the “Safety Alert Symbol” and followed by a “Signal word” such as “WARNING”, “CAUTION”, “NOTE” & “ENVIRONMENTAL NOTE”.

Your safety is involved when these words and symbols are used. Become familiar with their meanings before reading the manual. This is the safety alert symbol used to alert you to potential hazards including injuries or damage to your vehicle or other property. Follow all the safety messages that comes with the safety symbol. The following types of advisories are used throughout this manual.

TRow.TCell: Symbols describes that the operating procedures, practices, or conditions that may result in injury or can prove fatal if not carefully observed or followed
Indicates information that will assist you in gaining the optimum benefit from your vehicle and enable you to care more effectively for your vehicle.
Indicates precautions that must be followed precisely in order to avoid the possibility of personal injury and serious damage to the vehicle.
Draws your attention to environmental protection aspects.

Sect3.Title: Taking Care Of the Environment
Sect3.Para: Dear Customer,

You would be well aware that environmental pollution is one of the most serious problems facing humanity today. Air pollution owing to emissions from automobiles is a major component of the same. The emission consists of toxic gases such as Carbon monoxide, Carbon dioxide, Nitrogen oxides and Hydrocarbons. These have very bad effects on human health, such as headaches, dizziness, eye irritation, brain damages, oxygen starvation and lung diseases too.

At VE Commercial Vehicles Ltd., our Research & Development has always aimed at bringing out significant product improvements in order to meet the user and environmental requirements. The Engine which powers your vehicle is very ecofriendly, fuel efficient and meets the present emission norms. In fact, every vehicle produced by us is checked and certified as ‘‘Non Polluting Vehicle’’ as per norms prescribed by Government of India.

While we, at manufacturing and testing have taken due care in preventing emissions, as a vehicle owner and user, you would be required to help in addressing this social concern by maintaining the vehicle in good operating condition.
The following tips would be of immense use to you to derive the best out of your vehicle, at the same time serving a cause for the environment.

ListItem.Para: Keep your Air Filter clean.
Proper tuning of engine.
Correct fuel supply.
Use EICHER genuine parts only.
Use recommended engine oils & lubricants only.
Strict adherence to recommended maintenance schedule.

Sect3.Para: “ALWAYS ENSURE THAT YOUR VEHICLE IS SERVICED AT OUR AUTHORISED DEALERS AND SERVICECENTER ONLY”.

Let us together fight pollution.
Thank You

Sect3.Para: * Applicable for Dual Fuel Tank Models

ListItem.Para: For Cab models VIN number is located at the cabin above the driver side foot step showing the vehicle model and chassis number.
For Cowl models VIN number is located inside the cowl structure showing the vehicle model and chassis number.
Example: VEDX5*XXXXXX*XX*P/VEDX8*XXXXXX*XX*P
Stamped on the crankcase, at the position shown in illustration.
The hydraulic cabin tilting feature makes the inspection and maintenance jobs not only easier but also safer to perform.
Park the vehicle on a flat surface.
Chock the wheels as necessary.
Make sure the engine is not running.
Close the cab doors.

TCell.Para: Shift the gear to the neutral gear position and apply the parking brake.
Before tilting the cab indoors make sure that there is sufficient clearance above the cab (height clearance and in front of the cab (advance clearance).

TRow.TCell: Do not stand in front of or under the cab while tilting or lowering the cab.

TCell.Para: Before tilting remove all loose items lying inside the cabin for less effort & easy cabin lifting.
Do not start any repair / rework under the cabin, without proper locking & checking of cab-stay. Cabin stay must always be engaged after cabin tilting to prevent any accident.
While locking and unlocking the cab stay, try to keep away from cabin floor to avoid any injury, take help of second person in doing so.

ListItem.Para: Rotate the hydraulic pump (1) knob as shown below for lifting & lowering the cabin.

TRow.TCell: Clock wise direction for lifting the cab
Knob direction in center position for cabin lock
Anticlockwise direction for lowering the cabin

ListItem.Para: Turn the knob of pump to clock wise direction.
Insert the cabin tilting rod (1) in to the hydraulic valve (2).
Move the rod (1) up and down till the cabin is tilted.
Lock the hydraulic cylinder when the cab is raised fully with the help of cabin stay.
Unlock the cabin stay first.
Turn the knob of pump to anti-clock wise direction.
Insert the cabin tilting rod in to the hydraulic valve.
Move the rod up and down till the cabin is lowered.

TCell.Para: For safety prospect the stay kit (locking rod) to be applied at max tilt as shown below.

ListItem.Para: Dual Fuel Tank consists of two diesel tanks (Primary fuel tank (1) & Secondary fuel tank (2)). Secondary diesel tank is fitted on LH side of the vehicle.
Shut off Valve (3) is provided near the secondary fuel tank, it is used to stop the diesel flow from primary (1) to secondary tank (2).
Do not “ON” the shut off valve during fuel filling as it may lead to incomplete fuel filling.
Do not “ON” the shut off valve immediately after fuel filling.

TCell.Para: To use the fuel from secondary fuel tank shut off valve need to be kept in “ON” position.

ListItem.Para: Keep the shut off valve in “OFF” position as shown above.
Fill the both the diesel tanks separately.
The EICHER Engine VEDX8 BSVI produces high power, low maintenance and high life engines.
Clutch
Bigger clutch capable of transmitting more power without any slip and less operating efforts.
High torque gearbox & smooth gear shift for ease in driving.
Shifting arrangement makes it more smooth and less efforts causes low driver fatigue.
Tandem fuel tank contains both Diesel & Ad-blue tank. Diesel and Ad-blue tank placed closely for easy filling of Ad-blue and Diesel.
Blue cap as shown in figure is for Ad-blue filling (1) and other cap stands for Diesel (2).

TCell.Para: Ensure to fill Diesel and Ad-blue in their respective tank only. Wrong filling of fluid in opposite tank will damage your vehicle.

ListItem.Para: Slipper Suspension is another type of semi floated leaf spring suspension with enhanced load carrying capacity, reduced down time and enhanced maintenance requirement (Only 04 greasing points).
The system is designed to alert the driver for any obstacles on rear side of the vehicle within the range of 1.8 meter to avoid accident while reversing the vehicles by a smart alarm.
Buzzer for smart alarm is placed in right side of cabin as shown below.
Continuous beeps, if object detected within Warning zone (700 mm to 1800 mm from vehicle rear end).
Continuous tone (without interval), if object detected within Collision zone (below 700mm from vehicle rear end).

Sect4.Title: Engine over run protection
Sect4.Para: Engine failure may occurred in below mentioned conditions

ListItem.Para: Driving in neutral position during down hill.
Shifting of gear from high speed (5th or > 1700 rpm) to low speed (4th or < 1700 rpm).
To avoid engine failure we have mechanism / system which will not allow to shift the gear from 5th to below when rpm is > 1700.

ParaHead.Title: Do's: - For shifting high to low:

ListItem.Para: It is recommended to wait for 1 to 2 seconds in neutral position and check that Low indicator light indication in instrument panel as shown below.
Every gear has a specific range of rpm as per below chart.
Do not switch the range from high to low if you are exceeding the maximum allowable limit as per above chart.
Do Not drive downhill in the neutral position of gear.

TRow.TCell: Lighting switch, turn signal, dimmer, passing switch
Wiper/Washer, exhaust brake & hazard switch controls

ParaHead.Title: ELR seat belt provision on Co-driver seat

ListItem.Para: The E.L.R. (Emergency Locking Retractor) is a belt retraction device which locks the belt only if the vehicle becomes involved in a collision or come to a sudden stop. In normal situations, the E.L.R. allows you to pull the belt out freely. Buzzer for smart alarm is placed in right side of cabin as shown below.

TRow.TCell: Slowly pull out the seat belt. If pulled abruptly, it may lock. Slowly rewind the belt while holding the tongue.

ParaHead.Title: Double handle for easy entry and exit
Foot pad for accelerator and brake pedal

ListItem.Para: The ashtray is located inside of the right and left doors.
To remove the ashtray for cleaning, pull the tray upward.

ParaHead.Title: Map pocket on door for keeping documents

ListItem.Para: Door -The door lamp comes ON when the driver’s side door is opened, and it goes OFF when driver’s side door is closed.
Room lamp-With the ON/OFF switch (1), the light can be turned ON and OFF manually, regardless of the driver’s side door being opened or closed.
Use the sun visor (1) to prevent sun glare. Remove the inboard end and turn the visor to your side, and it serves as a side visor.
Driver Door
Driver window switch.
Co-driver window switch.
Co-driver door
Co-driver window switch (1).

Lift axle switch is provided on left side of the dashboard. Press the switch to ON position for lifting the axle manually irrespective of vehicle load/unload condition lift axle will automatically be lifted when the vehicle is traveling in reverse condition.

TRow.TCell: Don't lift the lift axle in laden condition.
Lift Axle position wrt. vehicle operating condition
While taking a turn, turn indicator has to be kept “ON”

ParaHead.Title: Smart Lift Axle position for Pro 6041 H

TCell.Para: Ensure Hazard indication turn OFF during vehicle turning and keep the turn indication ON.
If require to run vehicle in hazard light on condition use lift axle switch for lift axle up during turning.
Don't ON the turn indication in plain / Straight roads.

ListItem.Para: The switch is located right side of the dashboard.
The angle of the head lamp beam varies depending upon the load carried by the vehicle.
Head lamp leveling switch is used to select the desired position as per driver comfort. It can be done when headlamp lower beam is in ON condition.
Head lamp can be adjusted in three position by rotating the knob.
PWR: High Torque and Less Speed. Default Set up on Vehicle.
ECO: When Less Torque and More Speed Required.
ECO+: High Speed and Least Torque.
In Uphill Driving Irrespective of Loading Conditions & On Plain Road Fully Loaded.
On Plain Road with Partially Loading Conditions.
In Downhill Conditions Irrespective of Loading Conditions & On Plain Road with no Load.
Pushing the center button of the lever causes washer fluid to spray over the windshield.

TRow.TCell: If the washer is operated continuously for more than 60 seconds without washer fluid in the tank, the motor may burn out.

ListItem.Para: Turning the knob operates the wipers.

TRow.TCell: INT (Rotate the knob to clockwise direction from extreme anti-clockwise position)
Wipers operate once in every 4 to 6 seconds
LO (Turn further clockwise direction one step)
Do not run the wiper dry. It will cause scratches on the windshield. Make sure that the washer fluid is spread before operating the wipers.

ListItem.Para: Use this switch to park the vehicle on road in case of emergency. Move the left hand lever down to activate hazard indicator (1). All turn signal lamps start flashing during hazard indication. Lift the lever and release to stop hazard indication.

Exhaust brake (1) is a form of supplementary brake which reduces excessive heating of brake liners by reducing the rpm of engine. It also improves the fuel economy of the vehicle. It is required to activate while coming downhill. To activate exhaust brake, pull the lever inward direction.

TRow.TCell: To activate the exhaust brake the clutch and accelerator pedal must be in released condition and engine rpm must be above 900.

ListItem.Para: Rotate the knob to turn on the light switches.

TRow.TCell: OFF Default Position Rotate the knob to extreme anti clockwise position
Rotate the knob to clockwise direction from extreme anti-clockwise position
All lamps are ON except Headlamp
With lever in down side condition

ListItem.Para: With the headlamps in ON Position, if the lever is pushed down the headlamps will switch to upper beam. If the lever is brought to neutral position the headlamps will be in low beam.
When the lever is taken up, the upper beam will flash (passing), regardless of the lighting switch, and will go out as soon as the lever is released.

Move the lever forward or backward, and the turn signal lamps will flash. With the lever in the position 2 left side indicators will flash. If the lever is kept in position 1 right side indicators will flash, the turn signal lamps will stay flashing, till the indicator switch is turned off when the lever is released.

TRow.TCell: Lamps left ON will result in run-down battery, making it difficult to start the engine. Ensure that lights are turned OFF when not required.

ListItem.Para: Cruise control system used for auto speed control without usage of accelerator pedal continuously. Used for enhanced driving comfort.
Vehicle speed should be above 30Km/hour.
Press the push button as shown to activate cruise control.
Accelerator / clutch / brake pedal not to be pressed. How to increase or decrease speed in cruise control
Rotate the cruise speed adjuster knob towards ACC+ to increase the speed.
Rotate the cruise speed adjuster knob towards RES- to decrease the speed.

TRow.TCell: Speed not to be less than 30Km/hour.

ListItem.Para: Please ensure to switch ON the battery cutoff switch before starting the vehicle.

TRow.TCell: Min 120 sec of delay time in battery cut off after key off.

Sect4.Title: HVAC - Heating, Ventilation and Air Conditioning

ListItem.Para: A- Temperature control knob.
B- Blower speed regulation knob.
C- Air direction control knob.
D- Air recirculating / Fresh air switch
E- AC ON/OFF switch

The air temperature in the vehicle can be controlled by operating the temperature control knob on the control panel. Temperature can be increased by rotating the knob towards the red segment and decreased by rotating it towards the blue segment.
The HVAC system has a four speed blower. The blower speed can be regulated to any one of the following speeds by operating the blower knob center of the control panel.
The air flow can be changed by turning the knob to the desired direction.

TRow.TCell: Towards feet & windshield (Recommended for cleaning mist of windshield in rainy season or in areas of high humidity).

ParaHead.Title: D- Air recirculating / Fresh air switch

ListItem.Para: In recirculation mode, air inside the vehicle is circulated again and again. In fresh mode, Whenever discomfort is felt switch air circulation mode to fresh and air is taken from atmosphere and circulated in the vehicle.
Fresh air position is default position.
The recirculation state is indicated using red colour LED.
While driving in dusty condition.
To avoid traffic pollution.
To get quick cooling/heating as required. Whenever discomfort is felt switch air circulation mode to fresh.
The AC button allows the user to select AC switching ON.
The AC will be switched ON only if the front blower is switched ON.
The status of AC will be stored during ignition OFF and same status will be turned ON, after ignition is switched ON.

TRow.TCell: When AC is switched ON engine idling RPM increase marginally, to adjust to the AC compressor load. When desired temperature is achieved AC trips OFF automatically. The AC compressor gets switched OFF automatically when engine gets overheated. The AC is automatically switched ON when the engine cools down.

ListItem.Para: Blower provides air for ventilation in the driver cabin. Fan rotates by the blower motor which provides the air in the cabin. Air speed can be adjust by blower switch also the direction of air flow as desired.
The switch is located left side of the steering wheel on dashboard.
It has four position-
‘OFF’ position - Blower off.
‘HI’ position - Blower speed will be increased.

Anti-lock Brake System (ABS) is an electronic system and add on to the existing air and hydraulic brake system. It is used to prevent the vehicle skidding during wheel lock under all circumstances. The ABS modulates the air pressure in the brake actuator or wheel cylinder to prevent the wheel lock and provide precise vehicle control during over-braking. This enhances vehicle stability and steer ability under emergency braking.
When the ignition is switched ON the ABS warning lamp (1) will glow for few seconds and then goes OFF.

The ABS warning lamp will blink continuously when there is some type of fault condition with either the ABS brake system or the normal service brake system. Pressing the ABS switch will give us information (Blink Codes about faults of ABS System). It is recommended to take your vehicle to the nearest EICHER authorised Service Center if any fault is detected in ABS system.

TRow.TCell: When the ignition is switched ON the ABS warning lamp will glow for few seconds and then goes OFF.

ListItem.Para: Telematics are integrated hardware and software wireless telecommunications device fitted in a vehicle.
Telematics includes GPS technology for vehicle positioning and GSM for sending vehicle information to enable present tracking of vehicles.
Use of Telematics in transportation industry.
Driver detail information.
Tracing the location of vehicle.
Condition of load.
Condition of vehicle.
Detecting the speed, kmpl etc.
Real-time services for customer.
USB port (1) located on dashboard center console.
It is used for feed of driver ID information through USB drive (2) required in telematics system.

TCell.Para: For telematics detail information or services kindly contact your nearest authorised Service Centers or telematics representative.
For telematics activation procedure refer FMS user manual.
For the creation of driver ID, refer FMS user manual.

ListItem.Para: Music System is optional feature provided in your Vehicle. The primary use of such equipment is usually entertainment. The primary control device for an audio system is commonly referred to as a head unit, and is installed in the center of the dashboard panel between the driver and the passenger. The head unit itself is usually a multi-purpose device that houses multiple components in its housing. The most common components are a radio receiver/tuner usually with AM and FM bands, and a small amplifier for driving an audio signal to speakers.

TRow.TCell: Press: Mute/Power on Press and hold (more than 2 sec): Power off ROTARY Volume switch
Preset button 1 (tuner): MP3 random track on/off
Press: Select sourcing.(USB/AUX) SET: Press and hold: Enter setup mode
Preset button 2 (tuner): MP3 repeat track on/off
Press: FM/AM player mode AST: Press and hold: Auto store

ListItem.Para: A door ventilator is provided on the door on both the driver’s side and on the passenger’s side.
To open the door ventilator first open the handle by pressing button in center of handle and turning the handle in 900 anti clock wise direction.
Rotate the glass in 100~1500 in anti clock wise direction to get the fresh air inside the cabin.
Rotate the ventilator glass in 150 to 00 in clock wise direction.
Rotate the handle in clock wise direction button will automatically locked.
Foot steps on bumper for easy climbing single piece metal bumper for safety.

ParaHead.Title: Better leg room and space on co-driver side for crew comfort and more storage space.
Floor mounted pendant type brake pedal

ListItem.Para: Better spacing between accelerator, brake & clutch pedal.
Better engine cooling and heat dissipation.

ParaHead.Title: Open able ventilation grill on driver & co-driver side

ListItem.Para: Better ventilation inside the cabin resulting crew comfort.

ListItem.Para: Easy to fit accessories in cabin no modification required during body building.

ListItem.Para: Better accessibility for daily maintenance check.

ListItem.Para: Better visibility for daily maintenance checks.

ListItem.Para: Better accessibility for daily maintenance check.
Better accessibility & visibility for daily maintenance check activity.

ParaHead.Title: Fuse box location with better accessibility

ListItem.Para: Better accessibility for fuse box.
Before starting the engine, be sure to apply the parking brake
Make sure that the Gearshift lever is in neutral position.
Place the starter switch in ‘ON’ position.
Turn the starter switch to ‘START’ position to start the engine.
Engine should not be put under full acceleration immediately after start.

When attempting to start the engine at morning / after the vehicle has been parked for a long period, crank the engine first and it is recommended to run the engine at idling speed for a short time (3 minutes). Warm up the engine under medium speed.
If the engine does not start, wait for a while (about one minute) and start from the beginning.

TCell.Para: Do not operate the starter continuously for more than 30 seconds.
Do not increase the engine speed above 1500 rpm for about 1 Min. immediately after starting the engine.

ListItem.Para: LOCK In this position the key can be inserted and removed. Remove the key and turn the steering wheel until it locks in position. Turn the key from LOCK to ACC to unlock the steering wheel. If key is hard to turn to ACC, lightly turn the steering wheel so as to turn the key with ease.
ACC When the key is placed to this position, the engine will shutdown. With the engine shutdown and the key in ACC position, the electrical system for accessories is switched ON.
ON Keep the key in this position during operation.
START Place the key in this position to start the engine. After the engine has started, release the key and it will return to ON position on its own.

TCell.Para: Never under any circumstances place the key in any position other than ON while you are on road. If it is placed in ACC position, the engine stops and with the key in ACC position, while the vehicle is in motion, all warning lamps, instruments, and exhaust brake also become inoperative, which is extremely dangerous. This can also lead to troubles in the electrical system.
If the key is moved to the LOCK position with the vehicle in motion, the steering wheel will lock and you may lose directional control.

ListItem.Para: After running the engine at idling speed for a while, place the starter switch in the STOP position to stop the engine.

TCell.Para: If the engine is slightly overheating as after ascending a slope or after high speed operation, keep the engine running at idling speed for a while to cool the engine before stopping it.
Stopping the engine while it is running at high speed could cause failure of the governor.

TRow.TCell: Engine should not be stopped immediately after vehicle operation, it is recommended for a short idling time before switching off (min. 3 minutes).

ListItem.Para: When you park your vehicle, pull up the parking brake lever fully to ‘ON’ position.
To release the brake, move the lever down to ‘OFF’ position.
To ensure stable braking performance, depress the brake pedal gently two to three times well in advance.

TCell.Para: Excessive use of brakes will result in overheated brake drums and linings, causing brake fading which reduces brake effectiveness.
When road surface is slippery with rain or snow, avoid abrupt braking, as slipping could occur.

ListItem.Para: If air pressure reduces due to sudden leakage, one can stop the vehicle by gradually applying parking brake for one time.
When shifting the gears, depress the clutch pedal all the way.
Do not keep your foot on the clutch pedal when not operating it. This puts the clutch in a half engaged position, which not only results in loss of power but also reduces the life of the clutch.

A change in clutch pedal resistance can be sensed when the clutch disc reaches the limit of its service life. If a higher resistance than usual is sensed while beginning to depress the clutch, the clutch disc is worn to limit and calls for replacement.
Locking, opening and closing of doors from outside
Locking and unlocking with key.
Insert the key and turn towards the front of the vehicleto lock, or towards the rear to unlock.to lock, or towards the rear to unlock.
Locking without key.
With the lock button (1) pushed in and the outer handle pulled up then close the door.
Locking
First close the door then Push the lock button down, door is locked.
Opening
With the lock button pulled up then pull the inner handle, the door opens.

TCell.Para: When you leave your vehicle, stop the engine and lock the doors.
Before opening the door from inside, ensure that the reararea of your vehicle is clear.
A door left slightly open is very dangerous. Ensure that the door is fully closed.

ListItem.Para: When adjusting the driver seat, make sure the following conditions are met to ensure best possible driving position.
Pedals are within easy reach for their entire stroke.
The steering wheel and controls / switches are within easy reach for sure operation.
Fore-aft positions
Raise the lever (1) on the right side of the seat and adjust the seat to the most suitable position. Release the lever(1) to lock the seat in the desired position.
Seat back angle adjustment

Raise the lever (2) on the right side of the seat, and adjustthe seat back rest to an angle most appropriate for holding the steering wheel. Release the lever (2) to lock the seat back in the desired position.
Front side
Raise the lever (3) on the right front side of the seat, and adjust the seat front height up and down from front.
Rear side
Raise the lever (4) on the right rear side of the seat, and adjust the seat rear height up and down from rear.
Seat provided is bench seat type.
Push the lever 1 in back and fold the seat back rest in front.
To open or close the door glass, turn the regulator handle (1).
Swing the lock lever to the “Unlock” position (1) & Slide the window open by pushing the sash in the appropriate direction.

ParaHead.Title: How to operate PTO on vehicle

ListItem.Para: Keep the vehicle in parking brake ON condition.
Keep the gear shift lever at neutral position.
Disengage the clutch by fully pressing the clutch pedal.
Turn on the PTO switch.

ParaHead.Title: How to stop PTO operation on vehicle

ListItem.Para: Switch off the PTO.
Release the clutch pedal.

Position the steering wheel by moving it in the up and down and fore-and-aft directions. Ensure the best possible driving position, together with the adjustment of the driver’s seat. Pull the lever (1) in upside, and the steering wheel becomes loose. After adjustment, tighten the lever securely in position (2)to prevent it from becoming loose during driving.

TCell.Para: Never attempt to adjust the steering wheel while the vehicle is in motion.

ListItem.Para: Make it a rule to downshift the gear lever as soon as possible before an excessive load is applied to the engine.
Shift into a gear which you use for uphill and use engine braking to keep a safe speed. Take care not to over use the foot brake.
Speed of the vehicle to be controlled thru foot brake.Low gear jumping not to be done to control the speed.
Use same gear while climbing on hill and coming downhill. Speed of vehicle to be kept approx. same while climbing or coming downhill.
Do not run the vehicle in Neutral Gear during downhill.

These lamps are provided to warn the driver about any failure or unusual condition in different systems. If any warning lamp comes on while driving immediately pull your vehicle to a safe location & check for probable causes and take corrective action. Let us go through each and every lamp individually.

TRow.TCell: Message will appear on display screen with buzzer sound. Stop the vehicle immediately as it will affect the vehicle performance and contact nearest EICHER Authorised Service Center immediately.
Warning indication for driver related to any electrical / vehicle related faults. Visit nearest EICHER Authorised Service Center immediately as later on it will affect the vehicle performance.
Message will appear on display screen action need to be taken as per display message. If still the lamp is continuously ON then Visit nearest EICHER Authorised Service Center immediately.
Warning indication for driver related to emission related faults. It will appear continuously / blink in engine ON condition Visit nearest EICHER Authorised Service Center immediately.
Indication glows when the Ad-blue fluid quantity is less than the 10% volume of total quantity.
If the indication glows continuously after the ignition ON condition it indicates that battery is not getting charged.
Indication glows continuously when doors are open or not locked properly.
Indication glows when the brake pressure is low or any failure in brake system.
Lamps glows when combination switch is ON for right side turn
Lamps glows when combination switch is ON for left side turn
Indication glows continuously when the head lamp HIGH beam is turned ON.
Indication glows continuously when the head lamp LOW beam is turned ON.
Indication glows continuously when all lamps are turned ON except head lamp.
Indication glows when the parking brake is applied.
Indication glows when the vehicle is running in low speed range (1st to 4th gear)
Indication glows continuously when the front fog lamp is turned ON.
Indication glows continuously when the rear fog lamp is turned ON.
Sweet spot (Fuel economy) grey zone (Single thumb)
Not in sweet spot (Fuel economy) (Double thumb cross)
Water in fuel Visit nearest EICHER Authorised Service Center immediately.
Air filter restriction Visit nearest EICHER Authorised Service Center immediately.

ListItem.Para: Indicates the temperature of engine coolant.
It operates when the starter switch is in ON position.
The pointer should register below the red zone (85oC)during driving.

TCell.Para: When the pointer is in the red zone, it indicates that the engine is overheating. Immediately pull your vehicle to a safe location and take corrective action.
Do not stop the engine immediately. If the engine continues to overheat, have the cooling system checked and repaired. Do not operate the vehicle

If the overheating and coolant level warning light and buzzer operate or the coolant temperature gauge registers in the H region, stop the vehicle and operate the engine at fast idle until the coolant temperature returns to normal.

ListItem.Para: Indicates the vehicle speed.
Indicates the engine speed in revolutions per minute.
Indicates the fuel quantity in the fuel tank. The pointer continuously indicating the fuel level. F denotes full and E denotes empty

TCell.Para: Usage of impure/adulterated fuel may lead to severe damage and improper functioning of engine and other related components.

- (Dwn)-Used for scroll down inside the screen.
+ (Up-Used for scroll up inside the screen.
EN ('ENT' button)-Used for selection of menu and go inside the select menu screen.

Available in respective circuits. It operates when the starter switch is in ON position.
The pointer should register between 7 & 8 bar for graduations.

TCell.Para: Pressure gauges shows full reading when sensor connection on service air tanks are loose
Low pressure indicator (Red LED) These come ON when the system /pressure drops below 4.5 bar. It operates when starter switch is in ON position.

TRow.TCell: Never operate the vehicle with the warning light or buzzer on or when the air pressure gauge registers less than the normal pressure. The brakes will not provide their normal braking effectiveness or may become totally ineffective.

This warning light illuminates when the air cleaner element is clogged. A clogged element could cause an insufficient supply of air to reach the engine, decreasing engine output and increasing exhaust gases.
It shows the total fuel average value of vehicle in Km/L.
It shows average speed of the vehicle while running and updates at every 10 seconds.

TRow.TCell: Fuel average & speed updates at every 10 second.

ListItem.Para: During ignition on the lamp illuminates which indicates the system is operating properly. When the engine is started if no error is present, the lamp will automatically switches off.
Instantaneous fuel calculates current fuel consumption in liter / hour while engine is running.
Trip fuel calculates fuel consumption used in current trip.

TRow.TCell: If fuel data will be reset then it will start from 0 again.

ListItem.Para: It shows the Kms. left for distance to empty fuel.

The fuel coaching feature offers assistance to any driver no matter how experienced. Vehicle saves data such as distance, fuel consumption and the sweet spot for trips to improve the transport efficiency even further.

ParaHead.Title: Sweet spot achieved (RPM and load in optimum range)

ListItem.Para: Best fuel economy achieved, suggested to maintain the same driving practice with same Acceleration, Gear and RPM.

ParaHead.Title: Grey zone (RPM and load in range but not in bulls -eye)

ListItem.Para: In economy band but not in best zone. Do some change in vehicle speed or RPM to come in best fuel economy with double thumbs-up symbol and maintain the same.
Not in Economy range, suggested to increase the RPM by shifting to down Gear or increasing the accelerator, depending upon the road condition.
Not in Economy range, suggested to decrease the RPM by shifting to up gear or releasing the accelerator depending upon the road condition
Torque is too high to achieve the economy Suggested to release the accelerator pedal.
Non in sweet spot (Out of range of guidance)
No guidance is available.
