ParaHead.Title: Add ON Connector (Applicable for all CAB models)
Add ON Connector (Applicable for all Cowl models)
Tech Tool Connector (Applicable for all CAB models)
Tech Tool Connector Applicable for all CWC models)

ListItem.Para: For BSVI emissions, this truck is equipped with a diesel particulate filter. The filter significantly reduces the emissions of soot particles (PM). Sulphur-free fuel (less than 10 ppm = 0.001% sulphur) must be used for correct function. Fuel with higher sulphur content or mixing other fuels can quickly destroy the particulate filter.

The filter collects soot particles. A lot of particles in the filter means a higher exhaust back pressure, which in turn leads to slightly higher fuel consumption and the risk of unnecessary wear on the engine. To avoid this there is a system that removes soot particles by automatically burning them away. This process is called active regeneration and means that the filter is heated in order for the particles to be burned up.

The filter also separates inorganic compounds (ash) from the exhaust gases that originate from additives in the engine oil. Ash cannot be burned up and hence regular service of the particulate filter is required.

TCell.Para: Exhaust gas is still unhealthy on trucks with particulate filters. Ensure good ventilation and exhaust extraction if the engine has to be run in enclosed areas.

ListItem.Para: The exhaust gases become very hot during regeneration. Under certain driving conditions, for example, if the speed is low or when stationary, the temperature can become particularly high. For this reason, the symbol "high temperature in exhaust system" is shown in the instrument panel when stationary or at low speed.

TRow.TCell: Avoid driving or stopping the truck in places with flammable materials when the symbol "High temperature in exhaust system" is shown.

ListItem.Para: Some of the truck's components related to Exhaust system can become so hot that they constitute a risk of fire or personal injury. Pay attention to these components.

The cleaning process for exhaust gases creates a lot ofheat generation. Exhaust gases and components in andaround the exhaust system will therefore become very hot. The truck is designed to cope with this. However, there are several things to bear in mind:
Keep the area around hot components clean
That no heat-sensitive materials are positioned near theexhaust pipe outlet.
Oxidation of soot in DPF is called as regeneration. This involves very high temperatures. Regeneration will be done in two ways.
Automated regeneration
Parked (manual) regeneration

ParaHead.Title: Soot particles in the particulate filter

ListItem.Para: When the content of soot particles has become too high a regeneration of the filter is required. The soot is burned away with high temperature in the particulate filter. Regeneration is normally performed automatically.
Once the soot content becomes too high, a regeneration of the filter will be needed. High temperature in the particulate filter will burn away the soot. Regeneration must be started manually.

TCell.Para: Your driving style affects how often regeneration or service of the particulate filter is required. For example, avoid prolonged running at idling speed.

ParaHead.Title: Automatic regeneration of the particulate filter

ListItem.Para: Automatic regeneration will start if necessary. Automatic regeneration is only performed during driving and when the load is sufficiently high to reach a sufficiently high temperature.
When the truck is used in such a way that automatic regeneration may be performed, at this time no manual regeneration is necessary.

Automatic regeneration will continue even during brief stops. Idle speed will be slightly increased. Regeneration is Stopped when the conditions are not met, for example if the exhaust temperature is too low.
Regeneration is restarted when the load and speed are again sufficient.

Under certain driving conditions regeneration is not initiated automatically. This can, for example, be due to situations such as continuous driving (several hours) at low engine load or continuous running at idling speed. In these cases it may be necessary to start regeneration manually

TRow.TCell: Make sure that the silencer and the areas around other hot parts are well cleaned and that the truck is in a suitable location when regeneration starts. For example, do not start parked regeneration in locations such as in a tunnel, in a field or in the vicinity of flammable materials or gases. Do not stop close to other vehicles or buildings.

TCell.Para: Manual regeneration should not be performed unnecessarily. It results in increased wear and damage the DPF & EATS

ListItem.Para: AdBlue fluid level
AdBlue fluid low indicator lamp
MIL lamp
AdBlue fluid low indicator lamp
MIL lamp
AdBlue fluid low indicator lamp
MIL lamp
It will illuminates when the AdBlue® fluid quantity is less than the 10% value of total quantity.

TCell.Para: Driver should refill the tank and there after lamp goes off.
DEF fluid must follow ISO 22241 or DIN 70070 standards.
Usage of impure/adulterated DEF may lead to severe damage and improper functioning of SCR System and other related components.

ListItem.Para: It will illuminates when the DEF tank fluid quantity is completely out or less than the 2% value of total quantity.

TCell.Para: Driver should refill the tank otherwise engine power will be reduced.
Usage of impure/adulterated DEF may lead to severe damage and improper functioning of SCR System and other related components.

ListItem.Para: Immediately regeneration required without any delay to avoid any torque limitation and DPF failure

TRow.TCell: Throughout regeneration, ensure that no unauthorized persons gain access to the truck

ListItem.Para: Manual Regeneration will not start automatically. This action must be done manually.
Manual regeneration should be started whenever the message “Regeneration required” shown in the cluster meter
Manual regeneration can be started when:
The parking brake is applied.
The engine is running at idle for 3 minutes.
Accelerator, brake and clutch pedals are in released position.
Coolant temperature is @ 80Deg C (Accelerate the vehicle for 5 to 10 Min at full RPM - this will ensure exhaust temperature for regeneration.

Press the Switch (contains the symbol of DPF) which is fitted at right side of the cluster meter for 5 secs. Then there is slightly increase in idle RPM and green color regeneration tell-tale will glow, which indicates the manual regeneration started.
Regeneration takes approximately 45-50 minutes. It should be run to completion,
If any emergency, it can be interrupted at any time by pressing the accelerator / clutch / brake.
After Regeneration is completed, the engine RPM drops, the green color regen symbol goes out and a message will show for Regen completed.
After Regeneration is completed, the engine RPM drops and the symbol goes out.
If the manual regeneration is interrupted then the cause is shown in the driver information display and need to restart parked regeneration.
After Regeneration is completed, the engine RPM drops and the symbol goes out.
The gauge shows the amount of soot in the Diesel Particulate Filter.

In case of running vehicle in hazardous area like p e t r o l e u m a r e a / G a s s t a t i o n / c o m b u s t i b l e vegetation/inflammable goods loaded on the vehicle /vehicle is nearby mass people / combustible material/Fuel filling station the automated regeneration n e e d s t o b e p r e v e n t e d ( i n h i b i t ) b a s e d o n customer/regulatory requirement.
The inhibit regeneration switch is used to stop ongoing regeneration or completely avoid regeneration.

In case of running vehicle in hazardous area like p e t r o l e u m a r e a / G a s s t a t i o n / c o m b u s t i b l e vegetation/inflammable goods loaded on the vehicle /vehicle is nearby mass people / combustible material/Fuel filling station the automated regeneration n e e d s t o b e p r e v e n t e d ( i n h i b i t ) b a s e d o n customer/regulatory requirement.
Once inhibition active, there will be a warning message “REGEN INHIBITED” appears in cluster until switch off.

There will be a buzzer warning sound for 30sec to alert the driver that the inhibition “switch is on”. In case of Inhibition switch is switched on by driver wrongly, needs to be switched off immediately.

TCell.Para: Regeneration will not be active until the inhibit switch off. Inhibition switch is only recommended for special conditions where safety norms to be followed.
In the event of inhibition switched-on permanently in the vehicle, will lead to DPF & EATS failure.

ListItem.Para: During regeneration soot and organic materialsaccumulated in the DPF are oxidized
The unburnt inorganic materials that remains inside the DPF as ceramic ash.

Over a period of time and in different operating conditions this ash accumulates and occupies aconsiderable volume in the DPF there by exerts a back pressure on the engine. This back pressure has adetrimental effect on the efficient functioning of the engine. Hence it is imperative that the clogged DPF has to be replaced or cleaned by external methods.

TRow.TCell: REGENERATION INTERRUPTED If the message “Regeneration interrupted” appears in the cluster while regeneration in process that need to restart parked regeneration

TCell.Para: GO TO WORKSHOP IMMEDIATELY
Whenever the message “Go to workshop immediately”
appears then visit nearer EICHER Authorised Service Center.

ListItem.Para: Urea level low pop up message is displayed on LCD screen whenever the AdBlue level is low. Fill up urea.
Fill up urea pop up message is displayed on LCD screen when the AdBlue is required to be filled immediately. Fill up urea immediately

TCell.Para: FILL UP UREA
Whenever the message “FILL UP UREA” appears fill the AdBlue as per recommended in Customer end activity.

ListItem.Para: Incorrect Urea Detected pop up message is displayedon LCD screen with warning symbol indication & Urea level indication lamp will be displayed when incorrect urea is filled. Replace with EICHER Genuine urea.

TCell.Para: INCORRECT UREA DETECTED
Whenever the message “INCORRECT UREA DETECTED”
appears visit nearest EICHER Authorised Service Center.
To prevent vehicle torque and speed reduction, always use EICHER Genuine Urea at correct quantity.

ListItem.Para: Use only genuine AdBlue
Check and top up AdBlue if necessary, before start of any trip.
AdBlue tank to be drained, flushed and replinished with fresh AdBlue incase of vehicle parked for more than 90 days.
After switching off ignition, wait for 120 sec to cut off the battery switch.
Don’t use any non genuine AdBlue
Don’t start your trip with low level qty of AdBlue.
Don’t run the vehicle with same AdBlue, if the vehicle is parked for more than 90 days.
After switching off ignition , don’t cutoff the battery switch immediately wait for 120 sec .
Do regeneration as soon as possible, in the event of Yellow indication of DPF for regeneration.
For Manual regeneration, following to be ensured
Park vehicle in a cool, clean, dry and open place. Please do not park above dry leaves, paper, oil etc.
Leave the gear lever in neutral position and apply parking brake before regeneration.
Turn off air condition system (wherever applicable).
Disengage PTO (wherever applicable)
Please be attentive to the vehicle when this process is ON. Progress should be monitored throughout the regeneration process by driver or authorized person
Contact authorized dealer when regeneration getting off due to excessive DPF temperature & message “go to work shop immediately” appears on the cluster.
Inhibit switch should be in OFF condition.
Don't operate accelerator / brake or clutch pedal during regeneration
Don't operate any other electrical or mechanical switch in the vehicle during regeneration.
If DPF regeneration symbol is not going even after regeneration for 40 minutes, don't try to repeat the process. Kindly contact Authorized service center.

TCell.Para: Regeneration should not be performed in premises storing inflammable goods like Fuel Stations, LPG Godowns and all such places meant for storing infla mable goods

ListItem.Para: Be sure to switch off the engine before washing. This is essential to ensure that water is not sucked inside the engine which may lead to seizure of the engine.
Always use clean fresh water.
Wash your vehicle in the shade.

TCell.Para: Before washing inside the cabin prevent the electronic unit.

ParaHead.Title: Be sure to wash your vehicle when :

ListItem.Para: Your vehicle has traveled to a coastal area.
You have driven on road which are muddy.
Coal tar, soot, and bird droppings are deposited on your vehicle.
Your vehicle is fouled with dust and mud.
Wash the under body and the inside of the fender by increasing the pressure of water.
Completely shut all portions and clean deposits with a sponge.
Use car shampoo if the body is seriously dirty.
After deposits and dust have been thoroughly washed away, wipe with soft cloth etc., in such a way so that no spots are left behind.

TCell.Para: Take care not to splash water over the electrical parts in the engine compartment.
When washing the underbody, be careful not to get hurt by sharp edges of metal parts.
In cold areas, carefully wipe off moisture from doors and key slots after the vehicle has been washed. Key slots and rubbers can freeze up if there is water left on them.
Diffuser & NOx sensor should be masked completely while washing.
EATS muffler of loose pieces of substrate should not be washed.
Never wash the vehicle until Engine and EATS system remains cool

ListItem.Para: Wax the vehicle once a month or when the coating does not repel water well. Do the waxing in shade after the vehicle body temperature has fallen below your body temperature.

TCell.Para: Do not wash plastic and resin parts in thinner or gasoline as discoloration or cracks will result.

ParaHead.Title: Telematics Device with SOS Switch
ParaHead.Para: In line with new telematics device introduction, vehicle are equipped with SOS emergency safety feature which can be manually activated by the driver by pressing the system's SOS button to connect directly to emergency services.(After Activation).

ListItem.Para: Enhanced Safety for Operators: Especially useful in hazardous situations like a runaway vehicle, brake failure, or other emergencies where the driver needs to quickly disable the vehicle.
Emergency Response: SOS switch are integrated with systems that automatically alert emergency services or the fleet management system, ensuring a quick response to the situation.

ItemizedList.ListItem: Remove battery terminals. First remove (-VE) terminal and then remove (+VE) terminals. Cover open terminals with insulation tape properly.
Remove EMS ECU, IECU, ABS ECU and ACM connectors.Properly cover open connectors with tape to prevent entry of dust.
Use FUPD or RUPD as electrical earthing point. Do not use chassis, leaf spring and wheel as electrical earthing point during welding. It may damage components.
While carrying out the cabin structural work, be careful not to cause welding defects such as incomplete welding, undercut, blowhole, crack etc.
Cover all electrical wiring and hoses properly in order to prevent them from welding spatters and heat.
Protect instrument panel, steering wheel and other cabin parts from damage while carrying out structural work.
Body should not be mounted directly on chassis, always mount sub frame/ long member on chassis to evenly distribute the load on the frame.
Front end of the Long member (C) should be of progressive taper type (A) to prevent stress concentration.
Always prefer to place Balata packing between the body long member and chassis side member (D) to enable absorbing shock load on the chassis.
Load body should be mounted with the help of U Bolts and outrigger brackets.
Outrigger bracket should be used between chassis side member and load body long member
It is always recommended to put short cross member (B) above center-line of Tyre (front as well as for rear axle) to avoid fouling in articulation condition.
Stay has to be provided between body long member and side member to restrict lateral movement of body.
Do not reverse battery polarity.
Battery earth point should not be loosed/ disturbed/relocated.
After welding, reconnect the ECU and battery negative terminals in the reverse sequence of removal.
Do not exchange ECU with other vehicles.
Do not stand on Engine, Transmission, fuel tank, Ad Blue tank, EATS and electrical wiring harnesses.
Do not drill or weld on chassis. It may result in deformation or crack.
Cross members must not be fixed on to the chassis with the help of bolts and nuts by drilling holes in chassis frame. This weakens the chassis frame
Chassis cutting and welding is not permitted.
Do not weld on chassis for tail lamp brackets. Tail lamp brackets to be mounted on body.
Fuel tank cap and Ad Blue tank cap should be properly tighten.
Fuel tank should not be extended locally and not to be shifted from its position.
Proper accessibility to fuel tank.
Do not cut chassis.
Cargo body rear overhang should be as per owner's manual guidelines.
After finishing the body building, carefully check the electrical harnesses, hoses and other chassis parts.
Provide sufficient clearance for 1. Fitment and removal of air cleaner, 2. Various greasing points, 3. Fuse box, Relay box & VECU and ensure proper accessibility to wiring harness and hoses.

Wiring harness and Pneumatic connection / Fuel line pipe should be clamped with rubberized clamps. It should be away from EATS (Exhaust after treatment system) by more than 100mm and covered with welding spatter proof material during welding.

Sect3.Title: Cargo Body Mounting Guidelines
Sect3.Para: Follow below guidelines for U-Bolt and Outrigger bracket mounting.
[Legend: O - Stands for Outrigger bracket and U - Stands for U bolt]

Below table can be easily understand by above example. All dimensions to be measure from rear end of vehicle. Mount U bolt and Out rigger brackets on LH and RH side according to details provided in below table.
Below are the clamps to be used for clamping.

TRow.TCell: Pro 6035 29’ CAB Pro 6035 32' CAB Pro 6041 29’ CAB
Pro 6042 29’ CAB Pro 6048 29’ CAB Pro 6048XP29’ CAB

Sect3.Para: U-bolts should be used along with chassis stiffeners.
Minimum M16 size of U-bolt should be used for body clamping.
Tightening torque of U-bolt to be maintained to 5~6kgf-m.
Maintain below shown gaps for fuel tank and EATS with cargo body.

ParaHead.Title: EATS: Exhaust after Treatment System clearances

Sect3.Title: Electrical Connectors For Additional Supply
Sect3.Para: Add on connectors has been provided for Additional supplies. Use mating connectors for additional supplies from provided connectors.
Do not cut vehicle harness for tapping additional supply. It may lead to electric short circuit/fire.

TRow.TCell: Additional Supply 1. Pin-1-battery Wire;Colourgreen/Black 2.pin-2-ignition Wire;Colourwhite/Blue 3.pin-3-earthwire; Colou brown 4.pin-4- accessories-Colour-light Green

ListItem.Para: For cowl vehicle, Fas Tag will be supplied in cowl kit.
Fitment to be ensured by customer and dealer can be contacted for the same.
Fas Tag should be fixed on windshield from inside as shown in the illustration below.
For single windshield vehicle.
For Double windshield vehicle.
Please contact dealer for Registration, activation & initial recharge of Fas Tag.

TRow.TCell: Recommended Area for Fas Tag fitment
Recommended Area for Fas Tag fitment
Fas Tag should be fixed from inside
Recommended Area for Fas Tag fitment
Recommended Area for Fas Tag fitment
Fas Tag should be fixed from inside
EICHER Life Max Green Diesel Engine Oil OBDII HD
EICHER ECO MAX DEF/ EICHER Premium Diesel Exhaust Fluid (Marketed by Bharat Petroleum)
DEF Manufactured by PSUs (HPCL, IOCL & BPCL)
EICHER ECO MAX DEF/ EICHER Premium Diesel Exhaust Fluid (Marketed by Bharat Petroleum)
DEF Manufactured by PSUs (HPCL, IOCL & BPCL)

TCell.Para: Do not mix up different brands or grades while topping up.
EICHER Genuine engine oil is specially manufactured and supplied for EICHER vehicles.
Oil filters will also have to be replaced with change of oil.

ListItem.Para: Always use EICHER “GENUINE PARTS”.
All EICHER GENUINE SPARES come in special packaging. Please look for the following identification on the packaging for genuine spares.
Every parts supplied by VE Commercial Vehicles Ltd. is thoroughly tested and certified for assured quality.

Rubber parts deteriorate with time and usually become hard and loose their effectiveness. Therefore, for safety, critical parts are required to be replaced periodically. Have these parts replaced at your nearest Authorized Service Center.

TRow.TCell: Any trouble arising in the vehicle due to use of parts other than EICHER “GENUINE PARTS” may not be warranted. To guard against spurious spares, packaging is subject to change without notice

Sect3.Title: Daily Check Points
Sect3.Para: Pull out the dipstick; wipe off the oil from the gauge surface. Re-insert the dipstick and remove. Check whether the oil level is between MAX and MIN.Always maintain the oil level between the MAX and MIN mark of the dipstick.

TRow.TCell: Always check the engine oil level when the engine is cooled & with the vehicle on a level surface.

Sect3.Para: If the oil level is below MIN top up the engine oil as necessarily.
The coolant level in De-acceleration tank should be maintained between the MAX and MIN mark. If the coolant level is below the MIN line, add the EICHER Pro HD coolant till the MAX line.

TRow.TCell: Do not open the De-acceleration tank cap when the engine is running. Make sure that the engine is cooled down before checking the coolant level.

Sect3.Para: The clutch fluid level in the clutch reservoir tank should be between HIGH and LOW lines. If the level is below LOW, raise the front flap and remove the cap from the tank, and top up clutch fluid up to the HIGH mark. Make sure that level is not exceeded above the HIGH mark.

TRow.TCell: Do not spill the clutch fluid while replenishing. The fluid, if split on body and resin parts, must be wiped off immediately so as to avoid any chances of discoloration and crack formation. Do not use a mixture of different brands or grades of clutch fluid because it will changes the properties of the fluid. Make sure that clutch fluid under no circumstances is mixed with mineral oils such as engine oil etc. Make sure that the oil tank is protected from contamination. If there are foreign objects deposited in the tank, the tank requires inspection and cleaning.
If the clutch fluid level is excessively low, leakage is suspected. Get the vehicle inspected and repaired by an authorized service center.

Sect3.Title: Gearbox oil checking
Sect3.Para: Remove Inspection plug (1), (2) (3) and (4) check to see that oil is full to the brim. If it is not so, top up gear oil up to the mouth of the Inspection plug hole and check for leakages.

Sect3.Title: Checking and Replacing Rear Axle oil
Sect3.ParaHead: Inspection and ReplacementRemove Inspection plug and check to see that oil is full to the brim. If it is not so, top up differential oil until it overflows from the Inspection plug hole (1).

TRow.TCell: Do not check or replace differential oil immediately after driving. Park the vehicle on a level ground while checking oil level.

Sect3.Title: Power Steering Oil Checking

ParaHead.Title: Oil filling procedure and air removal

ListItem.Para: Remove the cover of oil tank assembly and fill oil in the tank. Small amount of oil go inside the system with gravity then crank the engine for a while the system level in the tank remains constant.
Then start the engine and give again half the throttle, observe the oil level in the tank, if it reduces again fill the oil.
Rotate the steering wheel to lock position. Do it till there is no foam formation inside the oil tank and in doing observe the oil level inside the tank.
Foam formation inside the tank indicates air in the system
After complete removal of air, top up the oil tank to the required level.
Fit the cover on the oil tank reservoir and tightened firmly

TRow.TCell: When checking the fluid level, be careful to prevent dirt or dust from getting into the reservoir. Always, keep the area surrounding the filler cap clean. Dirt or dust in the reservoir clogs the filter which will hamper the satisfactory operation of the power steering system.If the level of the power steering fluid is low or if the fluid quality is deteriorated, the power steering system may not function properly. Continued use of the power steering under these conditions will adversely affect the steering operation. When checking the level of the power steering fluid, ensure that the engine is stopped.
Oil level should not go below the MIN. level mark. If the oil level is low, hard steering or unusual noise will result. For oil change, take your vehicle to the nearest authorized service center.

ListItem.Para: If the steering is hard, check for leakages in the entire system. After arresting the leakage, bleed the system as mentioned below
Rise and support the vehicle with the help of garage jack on the front axle.
Remove the cover and pour the oil the oil level becomes constant up to reservoir neck.
Crank the engine several times till the oil level does not drop, if oil level drops continue pour the oil till its level become constant.
Rotate the steering wheel from left to right till there is no foam formation inside the oil tank.
If air is noticed, continue steering the wheel from lock to lock with engine in ON condition till there is no foam formation inside the reservoir.
After completing removal of air, top up the oil tank to the required level.
Fit the top cover of oil tank reservoir.

ParaHead.Title: Draining of water from fuel filter

Sect3.Para: For draining of water from fuel filter, loosen the water separator drain plug gradually (anticlockwise direction) then drain the water through drain tube.

TRow.TCell: It is not necessary to completely remove the drain plug as water is discharged gradually through the groove of the loosened plug.

Sect3.ParaHead: Draining water condensate from the air reservoir
Sect3.Para: Open the air reservoir drain cock and drain the water collected. Make sure that the drain cock is closed completely after draining.

ListItem.Para: If a large amount of water is drained out, there could be a problem with the air dryer, so have it inspected at nearest Authorized Service Center immediately.

Sect3.ParaHead: Tyre inflation pressure, cracks, damage and abnormal wearing
Sect3.Para: Inspect the tyres for cracks, damages, and abnormal tread wear.Check the tyres (including spare tyre) for proper air pressure with a pressure gauge. Always remember to put on the valve caps after inspection.
For setting the correct tyre pressure Refer tyre manufacturer’s recommendation.

TRow.TCell: Do not drive with incorrect air pressure.

Sect3.Para: Recommended Tyre Pressure (PSI)

Inspect the tyre to make sure that it has enough tread. Replace the tyre if the tread groove depth is less than1.6 mm. The tyres have 6 equally spaced locations (TWI positions) that indicate when the tread groove depth is less than 1.6 mm. Replace the tyres with new ones when the tyres have worn out and expose the marks.

TRow.TCell: Driving with cracked, damaged, or worn out tyre. Do not drive with tyres that have a tread groove depth less than the usage limit. Failure to do so could cause slipping and hydroplaning at high speeds.
Tyre tread groove depth usage limit

Sect3.Para: With the vehicle parked on a level ground and parking brake applied, walk around the vehicle and visually inspect each wheel assembly for the following:
- Loose, damaged or missing fasteners.
- Loose, damaged or missing hubcaps.
- Oil leaks at front hub cap.
- Lubricant on the hub, or inside the wheel area.
- Insufficient oil level and poor oil condition
If any of the above conditions are found, the vehicle should be placed out of service until the conditions are repaired by nearest Authorized Service Center.
There are many reasons for wear down of tyres.To achieve similar wear of all tyres including spare tyre, it is recommended to rotate tyres at every 10,000 km. or before as per illustration.

Sect3.Title: Air conditioning filters
Sect3.Para: - Open The front flap and take out the blower filter by loosening two screw provided on blower filter.
- Clean the filter by flashing water or by air.
- Open The front flap and take out the blower filter by loosening two screw provided on blower filter.
- Clean the filter by flashing water or by air.

ListItem.Para: Remove instrument pad and cluster LID.
Remove thermo sensor connector from thermo sensor.
Unscrew the filter cover from HVAC and remove filter cover then remove filter.
Clean the filter by flashing by dry air.

Sect3.Title: Battery

ParaHead.Title: Inspecting and topping up the battery

Sect3.Para: The battery fluid level should be between the MAX. and MIN. levels above the plates. If the level is low, remove the caps and add distilled water up to the MAX. level above the plates.

TRow.TCell: The battery generates hydrogen gas, therefore, any fire or sparks in the vicinity of the battery should be avoided. When a metal tool is used near the battery, disconnect the cable from the (-Ve) terminal. In case of skin contact with the battery fluid, wash the fluid away immediately. If the fluid gets in the to eyes, thoroughly wash away with a large amount of clean water and get medical attention immediately.After addition of distilled water, operate the vehicle to charge the battery, as there is danger of freezing when the atmospheric temperature low. If there is a loose terminal, tighten it firmly.
Keep the ignition switch “Off” while removing Battery terminal.

Sect3.Para: Clean battery terminals if corroded, if white powder builds up at the terminals, use hot water to wash it off. If the corrosion is very heavy, use wire brush and sand paper for cleaning.
Apply petroleum jelly to the terminals after cleaning.

TRow.TCell: Be sure to close all filler ports while cleaning so as to prevent entry of foreign matter into the cells.
Ensure that used batteries are not disposed of in any manner other than depositing with the dealer. Hence, please do not discard the battery in a trash bin or give/sell to any unauthorized person.

Sect3.Para: Turning the lever operates the wipers. INT (turned one step): Wipers operate once in every 4 to 6 seconds. LO (turned two steps): Wipers operate at low speed. HI (turned three steps): Wipers operate at high speed.
Pushing the center button of the lever causes washer fluid to spray over the windshield.

TRow.TCell: If the wipers are operated over dry windshield which is contaminated with dust and dirt, damage to the glass could result. Make sure that the washer fluid is sprayed before operating the wipers. If the washer is operated continuously for more than 60 seconds or when there is no washer fluid in the tank, the motor could burn out. If wipers do not operate when the lever is turned, immediately turn off the switch and identify the cause of trouble.

ParaHead.Title: Windshield washer level and spray performance

Sect3.Para: Open the front panel.Open the reservoir cap and check the fluid level. If it is low, add washer fluid through the reservoir filler opening.Check if the washer fluid sprays out normally. If not have it inspected and serviced at nearest Authorized Service Center.

TRow.TCell: Do not use coolant as washer fluid it could impair windshield visibility.
Do not operate the wiper if the washer reservoir is empty. Always keep the washer tank filled with fluid to the specified level.

Sect3.Para: Always use windshield washer fluid.

The brake valve operation is normal if the pedal does not stuck when pressed and an air exhaust sound could be heard when released. The brake pedal should return to its original position when released.

Turn ON each light switch and check respective light glows or flashes. The turn indicators are normal if they flash 60 to 120 times per minute. Check the light lenses and reflectors are free from dirt and damage.
Check for color of exhaust gases after the engine has been warmed up.
The color of exhaust indicates the condition of the engine.

TRow.TCell: Wrong, engine oil present or entered in combustion chamber.
Wrong, coolant & engine oil both present in combustion chamber.
Wrong, coolant or water present in combustion chamber or unburnt fuel passing into exhaust system.

Sect3.Para: AdBlue® fluid should be stored in the manufacturer’s original container and kept in a cool, covered area with good ventilation and away from direct sunlight.This will prevent water loss due to evaporation and ensure 18 months of product life.
AdBlue® fluid is non-flammable, but it should be moved to a safe location if there is a fire, as intense heat may cause the sealed storage container to pressurize and possibly rupture.
AdBlue® fluid freezes at 11.5 Deg C. When it Thaws, the product will return to its initial quality.

TRow.TCell: Containers and pumps with internal plating or paints must not be used.

Sect3.Para: AdBlue® fluid is a color less, transparent and harmless chemical liquid. Therefore it is a safe substance even if split onto the body or clothes. It has a slight ammonia odor.

TRow.TCell: For AdBlue concentration measurement EICHER Authorized Service Center.

Sect3.ParaHead: Procedure for Refilling of AdBlue Tank

ListItem.Para: Insert the cap key in to the AdBlue tank cap.
Rotate the key in anti clockwise direction and unlock it, then remove the key
Press the cap and rotate it in anti clockwise direction and take it out
Refill the AdBlue® fluid by using plastic funnel only.

TRow.TCell: Use only plastic dispensers or plastic equipment's for AdBlue filling since it is corrosive to other any type of materials.

ListItem.Para: After refiling properly relocate the cap into the AdBlue tank and rotate in clockwise direction.
Insert the key in cap and lock it by rotating in clockwise direction and then remove the key.
Ensure the cap is locked by rotating freely.

TRow.TCell: If the cap is rotating freely, it means cap is locked properly.

Sect3.Title: DO’S AND DON’TS

ListItem.Para: Do keep SRC system clean.
Do only use approved containers and pumps.
Do only use clean containers, hoses, funnels, pumps etc.
Do only use containers made of stainless steel (Grade 304 or better), plastics of PP (polypropylene) or PE (polyethylene) for AdBlue® fluid storage.

TRow.TCell: AdBlue® fluid will react with some materials, such as: steels, aluminum, copper, brass, zinc etc. causing contamination.

ListItem.Para: Do top up with Fluid before you leave for coming trips.

While AdBlue® is a safe liquid; it may cause slight skin irritation for some people. Avoid breathing the fumes, gas, vapor or spray. If split onto the body, clothes or ground flush the area generously with water.
Do not ever use contaminated AdBlue®.
Do not store AdBlue® fluid in direct sunlight.

If any fluid (for example with: Diesel, Petrol, Kerosene, Oil, Coolant, Paint, Rust etc.) other than AdBlue® fluid is put in the AdBlue tank do not turn the ignition key ON. Doing so will cause the dosing pump to prime the AdBlue® fluid system with contaminated AdBlue® fluid.
Do not overfill the AdBlue® tank otherwise it will cause splashing of Adblue and crystallization.

Do not add water. Use of impure or other AdBlue® fluid liquids like water may cause gradual degradation and subsequent clogging of the catalyst resulting in costly replacement of parts and reduced efficiency.
Do not use adulterated fuel or any other fuel. It will lead to clogging of catalyst.
Do not relocated the SCR system parts also avoid collision of parts.

TRow.TCell: Min 120 sec of delay time in battery cut off after key off.

Do not switch “OFF” Battery cut off switch till 120 seconds of turning “IGNITION OFF” to prevent exhaust system from damage. As the dosing system continues to operate for up to 120 seconds after the ignition is switched OFF, wait for more than 120 seconds before either disconnecting the vehicle’s battery (-) then (+) terminals or replenishing the AdBlue tank.
The location of all parts in the EATS system is critical for correct emission control. Modifications to the EATS system and its components, including the exhaust, are not permitted.

Do not fill diesel in tank, If filled accidentally AdBlue immediately drain the solution on and clean the tank with hot water (temperature not more than 60C). Refill with 0 fresh fluid any vice- versa. AdBlue® Do not do any adultery on in diesel. Any adultery on can cause serious damage to the muffler system.

ParaHead.Title: Do's and Don't s for satisfactory functioning of Electrical system

TRow.TCell: Before replacing a blown fusible link, make sure that the battery cut-off switch is in OFF position so as to ensure safety. Use genuine fusible links for replacement.

ListItem.Para: When servicing the electrical system, be sure to off the cut off switch.
Diagnostic connector should not be left hanging loose and should be handled with care.
Ensure proper connectivity of ECU/sensor connectors with wiring harness.
Check battery indicator and top up electrolyte, if necessary. Keep battery terminals clean and cable joints tight. Apply Vaseline/petroleum jelly on terminals.
Reverse polarity protection: care needs to be taken while battery connection.
Ensure the right connections of power cables like starter and alternator connections.
Ensure flashing of right DATA SET with reference to the engine hardware.
Do not allow any unauthorized person to tamper with engine settings or to carry modifications on the vehicle.
Electrical tapping not allowed. Tapping should not be taken as this can severely affect the performance of ECU and sensors.
Do not run the alternator without battery connection.
Mounting position of the ECU should not be changed.
No undue pressure to be applied on the ECU during the servicing.
While removing the connector from sensor don't pull with the wires and do unlock in the connector and remove it.

Sect3.ParaHead: Do's and don't s for satisfactory functioning of Lubrication system

ListItem.Para: Ensure that oil filter element replaced as per schedule Replace with only genuine recommended brands.
Check oil level in engine on regular interval.
Check (on level ground) the oil level and top up if necessary, before starting or at least 5 minutes after the engine is shutdown.
Use care to keep dirt and debris from entering the crank case through the oil filler port when adding engine oil.
Recommended to replace O-Ring whenever open the filter cap.
As soon as you see any leakage of oil in the engine, get it attended.
Follow low oil pressure warning on instrument cluster.
Do not mix different grade of lubricants during top up.
Do not overfill engine oil in sump. This may cause engine smoking and high engine oil consumption.
Do not use of thicker engine oil for higher engine oil pressure, it will only reduced engine life.
Be careful not to spill oil. When the exhaust system is still hot, spilled oil on the hot exhaust system can cause personal injury and/or property damage due to fire.
Do not spill oil on electric system. If oil is spilled, carefully wipe it off with a cloth.
Do not drive the vehicle with the oil level below the “LOW” mark on the oil dipstick. An insufficient quantity of engine oil can cause engine seizure.

Sect3.Title: Do's and Don't s for satisfactory functioning of Engine

ListItem.Para: During vehicle maintenance work and any welding work ensure that engine parts are covered properly.
During vehicle maintenance work and any welding work ensure that engine parts are covered properly.
During any maintenance work cover all the ports of turbocharger and inlet system/Exhaust system/water pump in and out so that foreign particles would not enter.
While dropping the engine on vehicle extra care needs to be taken so that engine parts are not colliding with the chassis.
Cleanliness of the parts to be maintained during any service work.
Ensure engine oil level before starting the vehicle.
Ensure coolant level on recovery tank and leakages if any.
Ensure all engine/vehicle harness connectors assembly for proper fitment and damages if any.
Ensure engine mounts and brackets as per specification with out any damages.
Follow the engine service schedule.
Use diesel fuel only, as engine is designed to operate on diesel only. Any deviation in fuel can affect the engine performance, emission and durability.
Do not apply external load on turbo-charger.
Don't step or claim on turbocharger and any other engine parts like valve cover etc.
Don't crank the starter with the external battery.

TRow.TCell: Don't step on valve cover or engine during the maintenance work.

Sect3.ParaHead: Do's and Don't s for satisfactory functioning of turbocharger

ListItem.Para: Regularly check all air, oil and exhaust connections for leaks and abnormal dust / oil / carbon build up.
Ensure protective caps on all openings of turbo.
Ensure pre-lubrication of turbocharger.
Ensure leak proof joints for oil & air. Check periodically.
Run engine at idle RPM for approx.1min. Before stopping & after starting.
Ensure that engine maintenance intervals are adhered to.
Ensure engine is operated in such a way that it is not harmful to the life of turbocharger.
Ensure all turbo connection hoses are properly clamped for no leakages.
Don't run the engine with low oil pressure. switch off the engine when the oil pressure-warning lamp starts glowing. Contact nearest EICHER Authorized Service Center
Don't put the engine under full acceleration immediately after starting.
Don't switch off the engine under full acceleration.
Don't run with damaged / faulty connections from the air cleaner to the turbocharger / turbocharger to the inlet manifold.
Don't run with damaged / faulty connections to the turbocharger turbine inlet and from turbine outlet.
Don't lift the turbocharger by holding the waste gate actuator control rod.
Do not store turbocharger in heaps, moist or wet place.
Do not expose turbocharger to dust & dirt.
Do not hold turbocharger by actuator rod.
Do not add any additives in lubricating oil & fuel unless until specified.
Do not raise the engine RPM immediately after starting.
Do not disturb actuator settings.
Do not run the vehicle / engine at idle for long periods (more than 20 – 30 min).

Sect3.Title: Do's and don't s for satisfactory functioning of lift axle

ListItem.Para: Ensure that lift axle automatically lifted when truck is traveling in reverse condition.
Do not reverse the vehicle until the lift axle is lifted completely.
Lift the lift axle in traction loose condition with manual override switch.
Always check state laws in all states through which you will be traveling for legal allowable weight and axle configuration.

Ensure that Max. cut screw (Wheel cut stopper) setting on lift axle as per specs.When performing maintenance on lift axle, ensure that air is not supplied to the air springs. Component damage, bodily injury can otherwise result.
When operating your lift axle, ensure that all personnel are at safe distance from the actuating lift axle.
Be careful during suspension opera on as suspension components move at a rapid rate.
Regularly check for critical fasteners torque's as per spec.
Use only VECV supplied components / parts / sub assemblies / fasteners for replacements.
Don't lift the lift axle in laden condition on.
Don't temper front / rear suspension.
Don't tamper pneumatic components such as pressure control valve, load sensing valve linkage.
Don't tamper pneumatic components such as pressure control valve, load sensing valve linkage.
Suspensions are designed to operate within specific parameters. Operating the suspension outside the design parameters may result in improper performance, damaged equipment, and void of warranty.
Don't over torque air spring assembly. mountings this will lead to failure of air springs.
Don't over torque steer damper mountings this will lead to crushing of shock inner sleeves.
Avoid direct contact of air spring and bushes with chemical solvent/fuels.
Do not use damage or deflated tyre on the axle.
Don't alter vehicle height by adding extra leaves/removing leaves from suspension or by any other means.
Don't tamper structural components by drilling holes or doing any structural alteration.
Don't alter the pneumatic circuit or valves provided by OEM.
Don't alter the electrical circuit of solenoid valve provided by OEM.

Sect3.Title: Incase of Emergency
Sect3.Para: Always be prepared for emergencies. Know where the tools are and what kind of tool you have. Also proper operation of the jack.
Tools supplied along with the vehicle

ListItem.Para: Tool Bag
Spanner (9x10)
Spanner (19x17)
Spanner (19x22)
Spanner (24x27)
Screw Driver
Wrench Monkey
Plier
Spare Wheel Rod
First Aid Kit
Fire Extinguisher Wheel Chock Blocks

Sect3.Para: The vehicle-borne tools are stored behind the Co-driver’s seat.

Use this switch when the vehicle is parked on the road in case of emergency. When the left hand multifunction lever is moved up, all turn signal lamps will start flashing. Lift the lever and release it to switch off the lamps.
The engine is located under the cabin. To inspect the engine, tilt the cabin.

ListItem.Para: Fire extinguisher to be held in an upright position Discharge Hose and hold firmly in the hand
Remove the safety sealing device & pull the locking pin
Squeeze the lever of valve, make sure that the discharge nozzle should be directed at the base of the flames toward the center of burning area.

Sect3.Title: Wheel Replacement
Sect3.Para: The installation and removal of the spare tire can be done by inserting the end of the crank handle into the socket of the tire carrier, and then turning the crank handle in the direction in anti clockwise direction. To remove a spare tire, turn the handle until the chain is completely slack. When installing the spare tire, be sure to engage the carrier suspension plate’s bar with the wheel disc. Also be sure that the chain is not twisted before turning the crank handle. Remember that inadequate tightening may cause rattling or dropping of the spare tire during driving.After installing the spare tire, pull the crank handle out straight, using care not to turn it.

Remove the spare tyre upper bolt & bracket from spare tyre carrier bracket.Insert the spare tyre cranking handle into the hole in the spare tyre carrier and turn handle counterclockwise, and the spare tyre comes down.To install, place the tyre with the convex side of disc wheel upward, mount the hanger (1) in the disc wheel hole, and turn the cranking handle clockwise to wind up the chain. After the tyre has been brought up a little make sure that the hanger fit into the disc wheel hole and the chain is not twisted.

After the tyre has been fully raised, tighten by hand to about 30 kgfm & remove the cranking handle taking care of not to turn the handle in the reverse direction. Check to ensure that the tyre is secured in position firmly.Fix the upper spare tyre bracket & bolt along with spare tyre carrier.

TRow.TCell: If spare tyre (LUG) has to be used in front, it should be replaced by the repaired RIB tyre at the front at the earliest occasion to avoid relatively inferior steering performance. If you cannot mount the removed tyre in position (spare tyre hanger), load it on the cargo deck or in cabin and ask your nearest Authorized Service Center for inspection and correction.

Sect3.Para: The jack-up points are as follows:

ListItem.Para: Front wheels-Under front axle housing
Rear wheels-Under rear axle housing

Sect3.Para: If the jack-up point is too high, extend the jack by turning the jack head counter-clockwise. Having located the jack head use the wrench handle provided for turning the jack nut by fixing the handle on the holes provided on the periphery of the jack nut.
Turn the jack nut in opposite direction with the help of wrench handle.

TRow.TCell: Use the jack only when replacing tyres.
Apply chocks to the wheels. If the jack gets out of position, it could be extremely dangerous. When you are using the jack, never get under the vehicle or shake the vehicle when it is jacked up.

Sect3.Para: Use the jack on a flat, solid ground, Avoid extending more than necessary.Strictly observe the specified jack-up point. The vehicle must not be raised with the jack installed at any other position. If the vehicle needs to be raised on the jack for long, use wooden blocks or square bars to support the vehicle.

ListItem.Para: Apply the parking brake

TRow.TCell: Pull up your vehicle to road side to prevent hindrance to other traffic on road and use a red flag or hazard warning lamp or use the warning triangle to warn drivers of other vehicles behind you.

ListItem.Para: Take out the tools and spare tyre
Apply chocks before and behind the tyre diagonally opposite to the one to be replaced.
(e.g.: Chock the left front wheel to replace the right rear tyre).
Set the jack at the jack-up point.
Slightly back off the wheel nuts with the nut wrench. Do not remove
Raise the vehicle on the jack until the tyre is just off ground. Ensure that the vehicle “is securely” held on the jack.

TRow.TCell: Ensure that the wrench handle is fully inserted into the wheel nut wrench and the wheel nut wrench is installed on to the nut all the way so that it does not slip while working.

ListItem.Para: Loosen the nut anticlockwise direction to remove the wheel.
When a front tyre is to be removed, chock the rear tire and when a rear tyre is to be removed, chock the front tyre.
Using the wheel nut wrench that is furnished, loosen the wheel nuts in diagonal order.

TCell.Para: Do not remove the nuts but loosen a little. Set the wrench fully over the wheel nut otherwise, the wrench could disengage from the nut, causing hazard.

ListItem.Para: Raise the side of the axle that is being worked on with a jack until the tyre is completely off the ground.
Remove all of the wheel nuts and then remove the tyre.

TCell.Para: When removing or installing tires, use care not to damage the threaded portions of the hub bolts.

ListItem.Para: Lower the jack and loosen little the inner wheel nuts as in the case of rear wheel nuts.
Jack up gently until the tyre clears of the ground and Remove the outer wheel nuts and remove the outer and inner tyre one by one.

TCell.Para: All the wheel nuts are of RH thread and to be loosened in anti-clockwise direction.Replace the wheel disc and rim if deformed or cracked.

ListItem.Para: Install the tyre to the wheel hub and tighten the wheel nuts in clockwise direction in the diagonal order.
Lower the jack and tighten them to specified torque in diagonal order.
Aligning the brake drum side notch with the tyre air valve, install the inner and outer tyre. Temporarily tighten the nuts and lower the jack.
Tighten the nuts to specified torque in diagonal order, making sure that the hub bolts are centered in the disc wheel holes.

TRow.TCell: Clean the tyre mounting surface of the brake drum and the inside of disc wheel before installation of a tyre. Take care not to damage the threaded portions of wheel nuts. Set the inner and outer tyre air valves at different positions

ListItem.Para: Tighten the wheel nuts in two to three stages in the diagonal order. In the final step, tighten the wheel nuts to the specified torque.

TRow.TCell: Make sure the wheel nut wrench is inserted all the way into the wheel nuts. If the wrench does not fit deep enough it could result in accident due to slippage. As an additional precaution when you apply your body weight to tighten the wheel nuts, hold on to the vehicle body for support and take care that you do not fall down. Store the tools and removed tyre and remove the chocks. After a short trip, check for loose wheel nuts. Also adjust tyre inflation pressure as early as possible.

Sect3.Para: The front and rear tyres perform different jobs, and they wear differently depending upon the types of roads driven, load conditions, and the driver’s habits. To obtain the longest tire life by equalizing tyre wear, rotate the tyres regularly, according to the tire rotation schedule.

TRow.TCell: After rotating the tyres, drive the vehicle a distance of 50 to 100 km (30 to 60 miles) until the tires properly wear in, and once again tighten the wheel nuts to the specified torque.

To rotate tyres, it is necessary to consider the direction of tyre turn. The direction of turn is specified depending on the type of tyre. It is shown by an arrow in the outer periphery of tyres. For tires on which the direction of turn is specified, rotates tires between the right front and right rear and between the left front and left rear.

Sect3.Title: Towing
Sect3.Para: Towing hooks are provided in front and rear for fastening tow rope.If there is a need to tow your vehicle:

ListItem.Para: Unload your vehicle.
Turn the ignition key switch to the OFF position.
Release the parking brake
Shift the transmission to the neutral position.
Use a qualified towing service with a suitable lift and towing equipment.
Whenever possible, tow the vehicle from the front.

TRow.TCell: Never attempt to tow a failed vehicle heavier than the dead weight of your vehicle or a vehicle which has landed in a ditch on the roadside. Oblique towing should also be avoided. It can break the towrope and may also damage the hooks

TCell.Para: When your vehicle is being towed, observe following precautions

ListItem.Para: If there is pressure in the system, turn the parking brake “OFF”
Keep the gearshift lever in neutral.
Place the starter switch in “ON” position.
Keep your vehicle speed below 30 km/hr.
Wherever possible keep your engine running while you are being towed. If your engine is stationary, poor braking performance will result.

TCell.Para: If your trip involves a long downhill road, brakes can overheat resulting in failure. Ask for a towing van.If the trouble is in transmission or differential, get a towing van to tow your vehicle with the rear wheels off the ground.

Sect3.Para: When you have run out of fuel, air enters the fuel system and you cannot start the engine simply by refueling. Bleed the system as follows:

ListItem.Para: Loosen the air vent cock a couple of turns.

TRow.TCell: Ensure that the drain plug is installed in the main and primary filters.

ListItem.Para: Position a draining container.
Install the air vent hose to prevent fuel spilling.
Perform the priming.

TRow.TCell: Perform the priming until the fuel flows without air bubbles in the pre fuel filter.

ListItem.Para: Tighten the air vent cock.
Perform the priming.
Tighten the air vent cock.
Remove the air vent hose.
Perform the priming.

Sect3.Para: If you have jumper cables, your engine can be started by using a charged or helper battery in another vehicle.Be sure to use a large capacity jumper cable, for the correct jumper cable, contact the nearest.

ListItem.Para: Turn OFF the engine.
Connect the positive end of the jumper cable (red) to the positive (+) terminal of the discharged battery and the other end to the positive (+) terminal of the good battery.

Connect the negative end of another jumper cable (black) to the negative (–) terminal of the good battery and the other end to the body frame of the vehicle with the discharged battery where the position is as distant as possible from the battery.

After connecting the jumper cable, start the engine of the vehicle with the discharged battery. If it is difficult to start the engine in a cold region, start the engine of the vehicle that supplies the battery power, and wait for several minutes before starting the engine of the vehicle with the discharged battery.
After starting the engine, disconnect the jumper cables in the reverse order of connection.
Use heavy-duty jumper cables for large vehicles.
Ensure that the total voltage of the auxiliary batteries is 24 V.
If the auxiliary batteries are in another vehicle, switch OFF the engine of that vehicle and ensure that the two vehicles are not in contact with each other.
Pay attention to the connection order when drawing power from a vehicle with a normal battery. The sparks generated during connection could cause a serious accident.

TRow.TCell: Avoid starting by towing or pushing wherever possible. It can be dangerous. Check fan belt for looseness and damage. Adjust or replace if necessary. If the battery fluid and fan belt seen intact, have your vehicle checked at the nearest Authorized Service Center. Make sure that the helper battery is of the same voltage rating as the discharged or weak battery. Never connect the positive to negative terminals. Never disconnect cables from the battery terminals with the engine running. If disconnected, a failure in electrical system will result.

Sect3.Title: Jump Start
Sect3.Para: When making a jump start, make sure that the helper battery is of the same voltage rating as the discharged or weak battery. For details, see “Run-down battery”.

Sect3.Title: Other Emergency
Sect3.Para: Should the brakes fail, shift down to 1st gear in order (5-43-2-1) and pull the parking brake lever to bring the vehicle to a stop.
If the water temperature gauge pointer nears the red zone, stop the vehicle as soon as possible and take the following actions :

ListItem.Para: Pull up your vehicle to a safe location.
Keep engine running at idle until the engine cools down.
Stop the engine as the pointer nears down to the middle.
Allow the engine to cool down for some more time. Then, remove the de-arration tank cap (1) and add water up to the high level.
Check the fan belt. If the belt is broken or worn out excessively replace it.

TRow.TCell: Run the engine while adding water in reservoir if engine is hot. Ensure reservoir cap is fitted back properly. Ensure that the reservoir cap is examined by Authorized Workshop at the earliest after over-heating is noticed. It must be replaced if required.

ParaHead.Title: Engine stalls while the vehicle is in motion

Sect3.Para: The brake booster may become inoperative due to engine stalling. Depress the brake pedal hard to bring your vehicle to a stop at a safe place.

ParaHead.Title: Engine stalls and does not start

ListItem.Para: If there are people around, ask them to push your vehicle to a safe place.
Contact the nearest Authorized Service Center for help.
Check for sufficient quantity of diesel in the fuel tank.
Check the voltage between the FIP solenoid switch terminals. It should be the same as that of the battery voltage.
Check battery voltage.

TRow.TCell: Do not crank the engine repeatedly if engine is not starting. It will damage the Fuel injection pump, stater motor and battery. This is applicable only in the case of solenoid operated fuel stop mechanism. If air is present in the fuel lines, bleed the system as per service procedure given.

Sect3.Para: If your vehicle is stuck in mud, do not accelerate excessively, as this will make the hole deeper and thereby make it harder to get out of it.Put gravel under the wheels to prevent slipping of tyres and move your vehicle back and forth by alternatively shifting gears into 1st and reverse.

Sect3.Title: Electrical Emergency
Sect3.Para: If a lamp does not come on or an electrical device is inoperative, the fuse has probably blown. Know the location of the fuse box and the names of circuits.
The fuse box is located at the left-most end below the instrument panel.Opening the glove box will help make the removal of the fuse box cover easier.To replace the fuse, proceed as follows:

ListItem.Para: Place the starter switch in LOCK position.
Open the instrument pad by rotating the given lock of lower cover assembly in co-driver side and lift of the pad.
Remove the blown fuse.

Sect3.Para: Make sure that the lamp bulbs are replaced with those of the specified voltage and wattage.Before replacement, ensure that the battery cut-off switch is in OFF positions.

TRow.TCell: Rear turn signal, stop tail & backup lamps

ListItem.Para: Pull the wiring harness connector from head lamp leveling motor.
Open the dust cap, unlock spring lock and take out low beam and high beam bulbs.
Rotate the bulb holder in anti clock wise to remove from the housing. Press the bulbs and rotate it to remove.

TRow.TCell: If you replace headlamps yourself, take your vehicle to the nearest authorized service center for focus setting.

Sect3.Para: lnsert a screwdriver into the groove on top of the lens and pry it to detach the lens. To remove the bulb, turn it counterclockwise while pushing it in. To reinstall, turn the bulb clockwise.

Sect3.Title: Introduction
Sect3.Para: EICHER driver training school is a special initiative taken by EICHER to promote road safety. Through its training school, EICHER provides dynamic training aid along with a team of expert faculty who educate drivers about right ways of driving. The institute works sincerely towards fulfilling its motive of “Safety behind wheels”. The driver training programs have been designed after a research. The courseware is constantly update as per the changing technology. To impart correct driving techniques the institute follow latest methodology such as - multimedia presentation, workshops, road safety films etc.
EICHER training program grooms a driver to tackle any situation arising while driving. Following are the advantages of the program.
By reducing road accidents a significant decline in the down time of vehicles can be realized. This results in increase in revenues
This ensures trouble free services from the vehicle, reduced maintenance cost and higher profits. This also ensures timely delivery of consignments
Well trained drivers and properly maintained vehicles result in an average saving of 15% in fuel consumption.
Well informed drivers take better care of vehicles and prove to be much more effective for the organizations.

In India, the infra structural insufficiency creates numerous difficulties as high technology vehicles have to travel on poor quality roads. Making situations worse the ill-equipped drivers further jeopardize the driving system. The EICHER Driver Training program has been designed to enable the drivers to effectively handle difficulties arising while driving.
The program trains a driver for handling following situations:

ListItem.Para: Inadequate upkeep of vehicles.
Mental stress suffered by drivers due to long driving hours and staying away from families for considerable duration of time.
Ensuring safe delivery of goods.

Sect3.Para: Tacking road hazards such as highway robbery / life threatening accidents.
Safety driving in unfavorably driving conditions.

To address the matter, three different training modules (Half day, One day and Two Days) have been designed. It covers all important aspects related to a vehicle such as - its upkeep, maintenance, minor road safety. We also have separate program for drivers handling hazardous goods.

Sect3.Title: Driver Training Modules
Sect3.Para: This Program has been designed for drivers who already hold licenses for LMV /HMV driving. This module has been customized for vehicle familiarization, proper operation, maintenance of vehicles and road safety awareness.

This program has been designed for drivers who already hold license for LMV /HMV driving. It is a customized module for vehicle familiarization, proper operation & maintenance of vehicle and defensive driving techniques.

This program has been designed for drivers who already hold license for LMV / HMV driving. This module has been customized for vehicle familiarization, proper operation, maintenance of vehicles, defensive driving techniques, on road assessment of driving ability and attitudes.

The course has been designed for those who already hold driving license but require RTO endorsement in driving license which enables them to drive special vehicles carrying goods such as - Oil/ Gas, highly inflammable goods and other hazardous goods. The program is designed as per CMVR norms and provides training for both defensive and safe driving. It also provide product knowledge and ways to easily handle emergencies.
To brush up existing knowledge of participants regarding road safety aspects and safe transportation of hazardous good.
Sect3.ParaHead: At EICHER we believe - safe driving requires right technique of driving.
Sect3.Para: For past 10 years EICHER has been development driving skills through EICHER Driver Training School. Here we provide Driver Training Programs which cover safety and fuel conservation aspects. Driver Training Programs are also conducted regularly at all our dealerships across the country. We actively participate in road safety seminars and National Road Safety week. Apart from this we have also designed a special driver’s manual in Hindi and other languages to make the understanding easier. The training programs cover classroom as well as practical sessions.To promote road safety we also developed three films on safe driving. The films received many significant awards and drew appreciations from one and all in the business.
Sect3.ParaHead: EFFECT (EICHER FOCUS ON FUEL EFFICIENCY & CONSERVATION TRAINING)
Sect3.Para: This module has been custom designed for vehicle familiarization, proper operation and maintenance of vehicle and providing practical demonstration of driving tips for fuel savings.
Sect3.ParaHead: During training the participants will be evaluated on four parameters

ListItem.Para: Behavioral.
Punctuality.
Hands on Skills.
Technical Knowledge

Sect3.Para: The first three parameters are based on faculty assessment during training. The fourth parameter i.e. “Technical Knowledge” will be evaluated on the basis of a test containing a few objective type question on defensive driving and vehicle aggregates and their maintenance. This test will be conducted before and after training to assess after training knowledge of the participants. The given parameters will cover following points:
Includes attitude of learning and candidate’s involvement in training.
Includes attendance and maintaining code of conduct.
Includes speed of learning of safe Driving Skills on different types of road.

ListItem.Para: Technical Knowledge

Sect3.Para: Includes knowledge on basic functioning of major aggregates of vehicle, pre-operational checks, routine maintenance, minor troubleshooting and defensive driving techniques.
To inform about the training schedule a calender is released on half yearly basis.
Interested candidates can opt for an appropriate training program.
Application should be made on the training enrollment from giving the required particulars.
The completed enrollment form should be sent to respective Competence Dev-elopment Center at the given addresses.
